word_list.word: it is easy to find a thousand soldiers, but hard to find a good general
word_list.pronunciation: ɪt ɪz ˈiːzi tə fˈaɪnd ɐ θˈaʊzənd sˈoʊldʒɚz bˌʌt hˈɑːɹd tə fˈaɪnd ɐ ɡˈʊd dʒˈɛnɚɹəl

senses.definition: used to imply that it is easier to find people who are willing to follow and do the work, but it is much harder to find someone who has the skills and qualities to lead a group effectively

word_list.word: an army of sheep led by a lion would defeat an army of lions led by a sheep
word_list.pronunciation: ɐn ˈɑːɹmi ʌv ʃˈiːp lˈɛd baɪ ɐ lˈaɪən wʊd dɪfˈiːt ɐn ˈɑːɹmi ʌv lˈaɪənz lˈɛd baɪ ɐ ʃˈiːp

senses.definition: used to imply that effective leadership is more important than size or strength, as a skilled leader can inspire a weaker group to triumph over a stronger one with poor leadership

word_list.word: (when|while) the cat is away (, the mice will play|)
word_list.pronunciation: wɛn wˌaɪl ðə kˈæt ɪz ɐwˈeɪ ðə mˈaɪs wɪl plˈeɪ

senses.definition: used to suggest that people will often take advantage of the absence of authority or supervision to do things that they wouldn't do if they were being watched

word_list.word: in the country of the blind, the one-eyed man is king
word_list.pronunciation: ɪnðə kˈʌntɹi ʌvðə blˈaɪnd ðə wˈʌnˈaɪd mˈæn ɪz kˈɪŋ

senses.definition: used to say that in a situation where everyone else is ignorant or inexperienced, even someone with limited knowledge or abilities can be considered superior or an expert

word_list.pronunciation: fˈɪʃ ˈɔːlweɪz ɹˈɑːts fɹʌmðə hˈɛd dˌaʊn

senses.definition: used to imply that when there are issues or wrongdoing within a group, it is often due to a failure of leadership or a lack of accountability at the highest levels

examples.x: The company's recent financial scandal can be traced back to the CEO's unethical practices - fish rots from the head.

word_list.word: a nod from a lord is a breakfast for a fool
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ nˈɑːd fɹʌm ɐ lˈɔːɹd ɪz ɐ bɹˈɛkfəst fɚɹə fˈuːl

senses.definition: used to imply that even a small gesture is enough to satisfy a naive or foolish person who is easily impressed by those in positions of power or influence

word_list.word: better be the head of a dog than the tail of a lion
word_list.pronunciation: bˈɛɾɚ biː ðə hˈɛd əvə dˈɑːɡ ðɐn ðə tˈeɪl əvə lˈaɪən

senses.definition: used to suggest that it is preferable to have some control or authority over a small organization than to be under the control of a larger and more powerful one
used to imply that people are attracted to those with higher social or economic status and overlook their negative traits in order to be associated with them or benefit from their power and influence

examples.x: The athlete's drug use was ignored by many because everyone loves a lord and he was seen as a successful and influential figure in his sport.

word_list.pronunciation: ɡˈʊd mˈæstɚz ˈɔːlweɪz mˌeɪk ɡˈʊd sˈɜːvənts

senses.definition: used to imply that treating subordinates with kindness, respect, and fairness leads to loyal and hardworking employees

examples.x: In a business, when employers treat their employees fairly and provide opportunities for growth and development, it can lead to a more motivated and dedicated workforce, as good masters make good servants.

word_list.word: uneasy lies the head that wears the crown
word_list.pronunciation: ʌnˈiːzi lˈaɪz ðə hˈɛd ðæt wˈɛɹz ðə kɹˈaʊn

senses.definition: used to imply that those in positions of power or authority often face great responsibilities and many challenges, which can lead to stress, anxiety, and difficulty sleeping

word_list.word: if you pay peanuts, you get monkeys
word_list.pronunciation: ɪf juː pˈeɪ pˈiːnʌts juː ɡɛt mˈʌnkɪz

senses.definition: used to imply that if one offers low wages, one is likely to attract and retain workers who are of lower quality, have less experience, or are less motivated to do their job well
